seoTitle: Mount French vs Mount Garnet — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount French and Mount Garnet. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount French vs Mount Garnet: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount French and Mount Garnet.
heading: Mount French vs Mount Garnet — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount French, Queensland and Mount Garnet, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Garnet has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Mount French (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount French or Mount Garnet safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount French has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Mount Garnet scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount French scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Garnet scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount French or Mount Garnet?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Garnet has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
